pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Preventing car accidents in India - Video

pageCaption.value: Motivated by the unnecessary death of his cousin in a road accident where bystanders typically ignored the victim’s plight, successful Indian businessman and 2010 Rolex Awards for Enterprise Laureate Piyush Tewari gave up his career to concentrate on helping to save the lives of his compatriots on the country’s chaotic roads.

WaPageTitle.value: Saving lives on India’s roads - Video

metadescription.value: Since winning a Rolex Award, Piyush Tewari has expanded solutions for fatality-free roadways to 15 states across India. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Saving lives on India’s roads - Video

text.value: Motivated by the unnecessary death of his cousin in a road accident where bystanders typically ignored the victim’s plight, successful Indian businessman and 2010 Rolex Awards for Enterprise Laureate Piyush Tewari gave up his career to concentrate on helping to save the lives of his compatriots on the country’s chaotic roads. Since 2008, he has worked through his SaveLIFE Foundation to train about 20,000 police officers and 10,000 citizen volunteers as medical first-responders and to introduce innovations including the Good Samaritan Law and the Zero Fatality Corridor − all leading to dramatic reductions in road accident mortality. Tewari’s work meshes perfectly with Rolex’s Perpetual Planet initiative to ensure human-well-being.

pageTitle.value: Grégoire Courtine's Life in motion - Perpetual Planet - Video

pageCaption.value: Neuroscientist Grégoire Courtine's revolutionary "digital bridge" technology could change the lives of those with spinal cord injuries. Learn more on rolex.org.

WaPageTitle.value: Grégoire Courtine's Life in motion - Perpetual Planet - Video

metadescription.value: Neuroscientist Grégoire Courtine's revolutionary "digital bridge" technology could change the lives of those with spinal cord injuries. Learn more on rolex.org.

pageCaption.value: Piyush Tewari is determined to save thousands of lives by ensuring that most accident victims in Delhi receive rapid medical care. His foundation, in partnership with hospitals and a medical science institute, has already provided training sessions in basic life support to over 2,000 police officers, as well as 500 ordinary citizens.

metadescription.value: Piyush Tewari is determined to save thousands of lives by ensuring that most accident victims in Delhi receive rapid medical care. His foundation, in partnership with hospitals and a medical science institute, has already provided training sessions in basic life support to over 2,000 police officers, as well as 500 ordinary citizens.

pageCaption.value: Billy Lee Lasley’s invention of a non-invasive method for determining the sex of birds — designed to help protect endangered species such as eagles — has slowly been adopted by researchers around the world. Perhaps more importantly, his work has proved to have other uses, including monitoring the reproductive health of women exposed to chemicals in the environment.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards Video - Medical alerts from a microwearable sticker

pageCaption.value: Biomedical engineer and 2012 Rolex Laureate Mark Kendall is on a mission to revolutionize modern medicine.

WaPageTitle.value: Medical alerts from a sticker on your hand - Video

metadescription.value: Scientist and 2012 Rolex Laureate Mark Kendall has produced microwearable sensors that provide critical medical information. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards Video - Eye test brings equality

pageCaption.value: Ophthalmologist Andrew Bastawrous is pursuing a vision to bring clear eyesight to millions of people around the world. Discover the video on rolex.org.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex Awards Video - Eye test brings equality

metadescription.value: Andrew Bastawrous is working to provide eye care to those least able to access or afford it. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards Video - Taking a volcano’s pulse

pageCaption.value: Eight hundred million people live in the shadow of fiery death, within striking range of one of Earth’s 500 historically active volcanoes. For Scottish volcanologist Andrew McGonigle, providing timely warning of an impending eruption is a goal that is both humanitarian and scientific.

metadescription.value: Rolex Award for Enterprise Laureate Andrew McGonigle has developed technology that could predict volcanic eruptions. See the video on rolex.org.
